qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Backlog batch: (F) commit.md:221 stale 'literal-task-id glob' label; (C) verify step-7 spec-continuation marker parity; (B) verify Write-sentinel three-candidate path + CF-1 overwrite-only invariant; (A) extend executable-context stripping to the remaining convertible danger-verb rules in pretool-bash-safety.sh.
qa.overall_assessment: All four items verified PASS with empirical evidence. F is a clean 1-line label fix; C and B are correctly no-ops (zero files modified, parity independently re-confirmed at the cited file:lines); A converts EXACTLY the two safe rules (dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred command-word-anchored at :1109, kill <PID> at :1320, kill being already in DANGER_COMMANDS) to read COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED while leaving every git/docker/systemctl rule raw and DANGER_COMMANDS unchanged. The hook was driven independently (not just via the dev-supplied pytest) for all AC-A2/AC-A3 payloads and the full suite re-run (57/57). One pre-existing, out-of-scope, security-relevant observation recorded (git reset with a quoted flag is not blocked by the unchanged raw rule) — it does NOT block this cycle per AC-A3's explicit scope note.

graph_verification.verification_note: No pre_query.json / graph_context provided in dispatch; graphify gates waived (advisory-only).

items_verified.evidence: Line 221 reads '(3) Mtime window + machine-readable marker predicate (final stage).'; grep for 'literal-task-id glob' across the whole file returns zero matches; the body at :222 confirms `grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->"` is the ONLY content predicate. git diff vs baseline f551a24 shows a clean 1-line replacement.

Producer spec-update.md:54-61 instructs writing '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <resolved-task-id> -->' once per (task-id,file). Consumer-prose commit.md:222 greps the marker as the sole content predicate. Consumer-script step7-spec-update.py:134 defines `marker = f"<!-- spec-continuation-of: {task_id} -->"`; :148 `if marker in content` is the only content predicate in _stage3_mtime_glob. Independent grep of all `task_id` uses in step7-spec-update.py confirms every other use is filename construction (stages 1-2), CLI arg, or trace string — NO literal-task-id content scan survives. Correct no-op (files_modified empty).

Pretool write-guard.sh:79-85 enumerates candidates `[writer_key=(env_task_id else session_id), data_task_id, session_id]` de-duped preserving priority. Posttool consume.py:230-238 enumerates `[writer_primary=(env_task_id else session_id), data_task_id, session_id]` de-duped identically. Both call match_sentinel_grant_for_write(candidate, session_id, target) (allowlist.py:523) with identical arg order — byte-identical derivation/order/dedup. CF-1 suppression at write-guard.sh:114 is `[ "$_WG_SENTINEL_RESULT" = "valid_no_match" ] && [ -f "$FILE_PATH" ]` — fires ONLY on overwrite of an existing file; new-file creation falls through to exit 0. Correct no-op (files_modified empty).

EXACTLY two rules converted to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED: dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred at :1109 (relocated past the strip-setup at :1091-1104 because the variable is undefined at the original :1044 — relocation is correct and necessary; placeholder comment left at :1044-1046) and kill <PID> at :1320. The 6 stripped-view rules are :1109,1116,1125,1149,1160,1320 (2 new + 4 original killall/pkill/kill-sig/rm-mv-workflow/rm). All git rules raw $COMMAND (reset :1414, push :1423, checkout :1381, restore :1400, revert :1481, stash :1360/:1370). All docker/systemctl rules raw $COMMAND (systemctl docker :1049, docker happy :1063, docker compose :1073, docker system prune :1080). bash_context_strip.py:26 DANGER_COMMANDS = frozenset({killall,pkill,kill,rm,mv}) — UNCHANGED (not in diff). Fail-closed preserved (:1091 sets STRIPPED=$COMMAND, overwritten only on ctx_status==0). diff to strip.py is comment-only ('four'->'the').

band_aid_detection.details: No threshold-lowering, error-swallowing, severity-downgrade, validation-skip, or check-removal. The dd-rule relocation MOVES a check to where its variable is defined (strengthens correctness); converting to the stripped view removes FALSE POSITIVES only and provably introduces no false negative (command-word-anchored / DANGER-command arg exposure). Fail-closed raw fallback intact.

standards_compliance.role_table_source: absent (infra/hook repo, no UI role table)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: The raw git-reset rule regex requires `[[:space:]]+` immediately before `--hard`. A command of the form `git reset "--hard" HEAD~1` (flag wrapped in literal quotes in the command text) is NOT blocked (exit 0), because the `"` before `--hard` is not whitespace. This is a PRE-EXISTING property of the raw rule, UNCHANGED this cycle (git diff vs baseline shows no edit to the reset rule), and explicitly OUT OF SCOPE per AC-A3's NOTE (the git reset rule is not converted and its quoted-string behavior is pre-existing raw behavior). It is recorded here as a security-relevant hardening candidate for a future cycle, NOT a defect of this cycle. NOTE: the spec's Edge-Cases worked example (ticket :122) describes the hypothetical bug IF the reset rule read the STRIPPED view (it does not), which is the rationale for keeping it raw — it does not claim the current raw rule blocks the quoted form.

Pre-existing failure test_allowlist_consolidation.py::test_regex_match (TypeError: signal.alarm(None) in the legacy regex-grant path at allowlist.py:59) — present on baseline f551a24, in the legacy regex matcher NOT the Write-sentinel three-candidate chain (Item B). Reported by dev-B as out-of-scope. Flagged for a separate ticket.

Pre-existing broader-suite errors (test_cp_checkin.py: missing hooks/commit.sh fixture + missing 'subtests' pytest plugin) reported by dev-A as unrelated to Item A. Not part of the context-strip suites; out of scope.

self_verification.notes: Every AC verdict is backed by either a direct file:line read, a git diff vs baseline, an independent grep, or an independent drive of the real hook (plus a re-run of the 57-test pytest suite). No claim rests on code-reading-only assertion or on trusting the dev report's self-reported counts — the pytest run and the hook-drive were executed by QA.
